channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parents of teacher found dead with 20 stab wounds offer theory on how she died after 'suicide' ruling reversed
item.description: The City of Philadelphia will reexamine the case of Ellen Greenberg, and her father believes investigators should be looking at strangulation as a possible cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian Siriano takes his New York Fashion Week show attendees for a glamorous ride
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In glistening gowns with sleek constructed lines, designer Christian Siriano pulled inspiration from his shared love of cars with his father for his latest New York Fashion Week collection Thursday.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US aid cutoff poses new challenges to rights groups and independent media from Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Newsom wants to speed up a delayed rule to make California homes more fire resistant
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — As Los Angeles reels from deadly January wildfires that destroyed thousands of homes, California Gov.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population infuriated the Arab world. It stunned American allies and other global powers and even flummoxed members of Trump’s own party. The reaction in Israel was starkly different.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: A hearing in a New Mexico state courtroom has devolved into chaos when two people rushed the defendant in a murder case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Egg shortage leads to odd pricing disparity with cage-free costing less than regular
item.description: The shortage of eggs has resulted in organic, cage-free eggs costing less than a dozen regular eggs in some New York City grocery stores.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter meets Eaton Fire victims whose home he helped save on live TV
item.description: “The ember cast from this blaze is just astonishing, you can see just how ferocious the wind is,” John Fenoglio said in his live report. “It looks like the back of this house is catching – oh my God.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Joe Rogan cheers Musk’s ‘business approach’ to government cuts
item.description: “What’s fascinating right now is we’re getting a chance to see what happens when you take a business approach to the government,” Rogan said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: 5 questions about DOGE's access to federal payment systems
item.description: Questions are swirling about how much access Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has to Americans’ personal data and sensitive federal payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: 13 states to sue over DOGE access to government payment systems containing personal data
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states vowed Thursday to file a lawsuit to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing federal payment systems containing Americans' sensitive personal information.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration sues Chicago in latest crackdown on 'sanctuary' cities
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Trump administration sued Chicago on Thursday alleging that 'sanctuary' laws in the nation's third-largest city “thwart" federal efforts to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Jose State and Penn face inquiries in Trump crackdown on transgender athletes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is investigating potential civil rights violations at two universities and a high school sports league that allowed transgender athletes to compete on women's teams, the Education Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: FLINT, Mich. (AP) — Chris Swanson, the high-profile sheriff of Michigan's Genesee County, is launching a Democratic bid to become the battleground state's next governor.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrest made in home invasion where men dressed as Amazon workers
item.description: The Savannah Police Department (SPD) has made an arrest in the home invasion that sent one man to the hospital in early January.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Daytona boss inspires students with his NASCAR journey
item.description: The "Super Bowl" of racing, the Daytona 500, will be the next biggest event in the world of sports, taking place February 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Davis Suppes, NewsNation

item.title: Superintendent forum interrupted by pornographic image
item.description: An online superintendent forum at noon Tuesday was interrupted by a pornographic image, according to a spokesperson with the Liberty County School System.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cost to sequester Megan Boswell jury thousands per day
item.description: "That's one way they can serve their country is to take their jury duty," explained Circuit Court Clerk Bobby Russell. "And that is a very important service.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, NewsNation

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, ABC 4

item.title: 10 witnesses take stand in Day 2 of Megan Boswell trial
item.description: Disclaimer: Some evidence that may be shown during the trial could be graphic in nature. SULLIVAN COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) - The second day of Megan Boswell's murder trial began with Judge Jim Goodwin's answer to a dispute that sent the jury to their hotel early on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy shares conditions for ending Ukraine-Russia war and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Second type of bird flu detected in US dairy cows
item.description: Dairy cattle in Nevada have been infected with a new type of bird flu that's different from the version that has spread in U.S. herds since last year, Agriculture Department officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New NASA imagery shows a large asteroid could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Celebrities rail against Trump's policies
item.description: After years of launching blistering attacks on President Donald Trump, much of Hollywood appears to be casting him in a new "Harry Potter"-inspired role: He Who Must Not Be Named.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4
